description: Extract implicit domain logic from ServerViewModel and MainAppViewModel into pure, testable domain classes in Fennec.App/Domain/

userStories.title: Extract MessageGrouper — eliminate duplicated grouping logic
userStories.description: As a developer, I want message grouping rules in one place so LoadMessagesAsync and BuildMessageItem stop duplicating the same show-author / show-time-separator logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Fennec.App/Domain/MessageGrouper.cs introduced — pure static class, no Avalonia or CommunityToolkit.Mvvm dependency

MessageGrouper.ShouldShowAuthor(Instant? previous, Guid? previousAuthorId, Instant? current, Guid currentAuthorId) returns true when author differs, OR time gap >= 5 minutes, OR a calendar-day boundary is crossed
MessageGrouper.ShouldShowTimeSeparator(Instant? previous, Instant? current) returns true when a calendar-day boundary is crossed between the two timestamps
LoadMessagesAsync in ServerViewModel uses MessageGrouper — inline grouping logic removed
BuildMessageItem in ServerViewModel uses MessageGrouper — inline grouping logic removed

Unit tests in Fennec.App.Tests/Domain/MessageGrouperTests.cs: same author within 5 min → no header; same author after 5 min → header; different author same minute → header; new day → separator; same day → no separator; first message (previous is null) → header, no separator
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Extract ShortcodeReplacer — promote internal static to domain class
userStories.description: As a developer, I want shortcode replacement to live in a named domain class so it is testable, discoverable, and no longer hidden as internal static on ServerViewModel.
acceptanceCriteria: Fennec.App/Domain/ShortcodeReplacer.cs introduced — pure static class, no Avalonia or CommunityToolkit.Mvvm dependency
ShortcodeReplacer.Replace(string text) replaces :shortcode: tokens with Unicode emoji using EmojiDatabase.ByShortcode, leaving unknown shortcodes untouched
ServerViewModel.ReplaceShortcodes deleted; call site in SendMessage updated to ShortcodeReplacer.Replace(...)

Unit tests in Fennec.App.Tests/Domain/ShortcodeReplacerTests.cs: known shortcode replaced with correct Unicode; unknown shortcode left as-is; multiple shortcodes in one string; empty string returned unchanged
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Extract MessageLengthPolicy — replace magic numbers with a domain concept
userStories.description: As a developer, I want message length rules expressed as a named policy so MaxMessageLength and CharCountVisibleThreshold are not magic numbers on a ViewModel.
acceptanceCriteria: Fennec.App/Domain/MessageLengthPolicy.cs introduced — pure static class, no Avalonia or CommunityToolkit.Mvvm dependency
MessageLengthPolicy.MaxLength constant = 10000
MessageLengthPolicy.CounterVisibleThreshold constant = 9000
MessageLengthPolicy.CharsRemaining(string text) returns MaxLength - text.Length
MessageLengthPolicy.ShouldShowCounter(string text) returns true when text.Length >= CounterVisibleThreshold
MessageLengthPolicy.IsOverLimit(string text) returns true when text.Length > MaxLength
ServerViewModel computed properties MessageCharsRemaining, ShowCharCount, IsOverLimit delegate to MessageLengthPolicy
Magic number literals 10_000 and 9_000 removed from ServerViewModel

Unit tests in Fennec.App.Tests/Domain/MessageLengthPolicyTests.cs: empty string not over limit; exactly MaxLength not over limit; MaxLength+1 is over limit; CounterVisibleThreshold-1 does not show counter; CounterVisibleThreshold shows counter; CharsRemaining returns correct value
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Extract OutgoingMessageState — make the optimistic-update state machine explicit
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the pending/delivered/failed states of an outgoing message to be an explicit sealed class hierarchy so the state machine in SendMessage() is not inferred from flag combinations on MessageItem.
acceptanceCriteria: Fennec.App/Domain/OutgoingMessageState.cs introduced with sealed classes: OutgoingMessageState (abstract base), PendingState, DeliveredState, FailedState(string Reason) — no Avalonia or CommunityToolkit.Mvvm dependency
MessageItem gains OutgoingMessageState? SendState property
MessageItem.IsPending computed as SendState is PendingState
MessageItem.IsSendFailed computed as SendState is FailedState
IsPending and IsSendFailed remain as properties (not removed) so existing View bindings continue to work

SendMessage() in ServerViewModel sets optimistic.SendState = new PendingState() on add, new DeliveredState() on success, new FailedState("Send failed") on exception — no direct assignment of IsPending or IsSendFailed

Unit tests in Fennec.App.Tests/Domain/OutgoingMessageStateTests.cs: new MessageItem has null SendState; PendingState makes IsPending true and IsSendFailed false; DeliveredState makes both false; FailedState makes IsSendFailed true and IsPending false; FailedState carries Reason string
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Deduplicate ServerRoute construction in MainAppViewModel
userStories.description: As a developer, I want ServerRoute creation to go through a single private factory method so the three identical inline constructions in MainAppViewModel are not a maintenance hazard.
acceptanceCriteria: Private CreateServerRoute(Guid serverId, string serverName, string instanceUrl) method introduced in MainAppViewModel

All three call sites replaced: NavigateToServerAsync, LoadServersAndNavigateToServerAsync, and IVoiceChannelNavigator.NavigateToVoiceChannelAsync each call CreateServerRoute(...) instead of new ServerRoute(...) inline
No behavioural change — parameters passed are identical to the current inline constructions

Unit tests in Fennec.App.Tests/ViewModels/MainAppViewModelTests.cs (extend existing): navigating to a server via NavigateToServerCommand produces a ServerRoute with the correct ServerId and InstanceUrl
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
